speaker: BRUTUS
text: Stand ho!

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: Give the word, ho, and stand!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What now, Lucilius, is Cassius near?

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: He is at hand, and Pindarus is come

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: To do you salutation from his master.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He greets me well.—Your master, Pindarus,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: In his own change or by ill officers,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Hath given me some worthy cause to wish

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Things done undone, but if he be at hand

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I shall be satisfied.

speaker: PINDARUS
text: I do not doubt

speaker: PINDARUS
text: But that my noble master will appear

speaker: PINDARUS
text: Such as he is, full of regard and honor.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He is not doubted.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: A word, Lucilius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: How he received you. Let me be resolved.

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: With courtesy and with respect enough,

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: But not with such familiar instances

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: Nor with such free and friendly conference

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: As he hath used of old.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Thou hast described

speaker: BRUTUS
text: A hot friend cooling. Ever note, Lucilius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When love begins to sicken and decay

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It useth an enforcèd ceremony.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: There are no tricks in plain and simple faith;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But hollow men, like horses hot at hand,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Make gallant show and promise of their mettle,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But when they should endure the bloody spur,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: They fall their crests and, like deceitful jades,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Sink in the trial. Comes his army on?

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: They mean this night in Sardis to be quartered.

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: The greater part, the horse in general,

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: Are come with Cassius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Hark, he is arrived.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: March gently on to meet him.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Stand ho!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Stand ho! Speak the word along.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: Stand!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SOLDIER
text: Stand!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SOLDIER
text: Stand!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Most noble brother, you have done me wrong.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Judge me, you gods! Wrong I mine enemies?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And if not so, how should I wrong a brother?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Brutus, this sober form of yours hides wrongs,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And when you do them—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Cassius, be content.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Speak your griefs softly. I do know you well.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Before the eyes of both our armies here

speaker: BRUTUS
text: (Which should perceive nothing but love from us),

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let us not wrangle. Bid them move away.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Then in my tent, Cassius, enlarge your griefs,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And I will give you audience.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Pindarus,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Bid our commanders lead their charges off

speaker: CASSIUS
text: A little from this ground.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lucius, do you the like, and let no man

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Come to our tent till we have done our conference.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let Lucilius and Titinius guard our door.
